name: Crop Production and Management
subject: biology
summary: How food grows — soil prep, sowing, irrigation, manures vs fertilisers, and protecting crops from weeds and pests.

crushed.title: Agriculture = crop production
crushed.text: Same piece of land prepared, sown, irrigated, and harvested year after year.

crushed.title: Soil preparation first
crushed.text: Ploughing loosens soil for air & roots. Levelling for uniform irrigation. Manuring adds nutrients.

crushed.title: Irrigation methods
crushed.text: Drip (best — saves water), sprinkler, chain pump, rahat (animal-powered).

crushed.title: Manure vs fertiliser
crushed.text: Manure = organic, slow, improves soil. Fertiliser = chemical, fast, can harm soil if overused.

crushed.title: Weeding & harvesting
crushed.text: Weeds compete for nutrients — remove by khurpi or weedicides. Harvest when crop matures.

items.label: Sowing
items.detail: Quality seeds, proper spacing, right depth

items.label: Irrigation
items.detail: Regular water supply at right intervals

items.label: Weeding
items.detail: Remove unwanted plants competing with crop

items.label: Harvesting & storage
items.detail: Cut crop at maturity, dry and store safely

right.label: Fertiliser (Chemical)
points: Can reduce soil fertility if overused

flows.title: Kharif Crop Cycle (Monsoon Season)
flows.description: Follow rice from soil preparation to harvest.

steps.label: June
steps.description: Fields ploughed and flooded with monsoon rain.

steps.label: Sowing
steps.description: Pre-grown seedlings planted in flooded fields.

steps.label: Growth
steps.description: Water level maintained; weeds removed regularly.

steps.label: October
steps.description: Golden rice stalks cut and threshed.

steps.label: Storage
steps.description: Grain dried to prevent fungal growth in granaries.

keyTerms.definition: Monsoon-season crops sown in June-July — rice, maize, cotton
Winter-season crops sown in October-November — wheat, gram, pea
Removing unwanted plants that compete with the crop
keyTerms.analogy: Kicking freeloaders out of the crop's lunch

keyTerms.definition: Separating grain from chaff using wind
keyTerms.analogy: Light chaff blows away, heavy grain stays

demos.title: Irrigation & Crop Growth
demos.description: Water reaches the field through channels; seeds germinate and shoots grow.

steps.label: Water released
steps.description: Canal water flows into furrows between crop rows.

steps.label: Soil absorbs water
steps.description: Moisture reaches seed coats — imbibition begins.

steps.label: Germination
steps.description: Roots anchor and green shoots rise above the soil.

chapterSummary.overview: Agriculture feeds the nation. This chapter explains how farmers prepare soil, select seeds, sow crops, add manure and fertilisers, irrigate fields, protect crops from weeds and pests, and harvest and store grain — the complete crop production cycle.
keyPoints: Good soil preparation (ploughing, levelling, manuring) gives roots air, water, and nutrients.
Quality seeds with high germination rate are sown by broadcasting, drilling, or transplantation.
Manure improves soil structure; fertilisers (NPK) give quick nutrients but overuse harms soil.
Irrigation methods include wells, canals, rivers, tanks, and modern drip/sprinkler systems.
Weeding removes unwanted plants; pesticides control insects, fungi, and rodents.
chapterSummary.examFocus: Compare manure vs fertilisers. Name irrigation methods. Explain why crop rotation and mixed cropping improve yield.
